tax: Placed at order rank, Metalline Elementals is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a native or precipitated metal body, not a stone, crystal, or sediment one. A new family belongs here only if it shows a distinct metal with its own ore source and attack mode.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a metallic coil or a rust-red magnetic mass moving against the ore-grain. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — a verdigris smear and arc-scorch, or a rust-bleed and clinging iron filings — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Metalline Elementals occupies copper-seamed galleries and iron-stained bogs. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where earth-charge or earth-pressure animates an ore-seam or iron bloom and recur with the seam re-grows or the bog re-precipitates metal, sustaining themselves on earth-charge through conductive ore, or earth-pressure in iron mud.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: members pose as exposed ore and rouse to metal tools or wet footfall, answering with current or magnetic mass. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: a coherent metal body, immunity to its own mode (electricity or magnetism), and a chemical or thermal failure. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each family and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: electrical discharge and constriction, or magnetic mass-attack and tool-stripping. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Metalline Elementals matters because its bodies are literally ore, drawing Forgemaster crews into galleries and bogs that kill them. It lets survey reports sort metal hazards by mode, so a crew knows whether to go dry-shod or strip their iron without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Metalline Elementals is the order-rank grouping of elementals whose bodies are native or precipitated metal drawn from ore within the True Elementals. Its members share a body of coherent metal — ductile copper or porous iron — animated from an ore-seam or iron bog, and divide below into family groups by the metal and its mode of harm — conduction or magnetic mass. The Metalline order is the Forgemasters' obsession, since its bodies are walking ore.
